articles.title: Patients in Deprived Areas Wait Longer for NHS Treatment
articles.excerpt: New figures reveal that patients in deprived areas of Rochdale are experiencing longer wait times for NHS treatment compared to those in more affluent regions.
articles.content_html:
New figures have revealed that patients in deprived areas of Rochdale are waiting longer for NHS treatment than those in more affluent regions. The data indicates a growing disparity in access to timely healthcare services.

Individuals living in less affluent neighbourhoods face increased waiting times for essential medical procedures and consultations. This trend raises concerns about health inequalities within the community.

Health officials have noted that the situation may be exacerbated by factors such as staffing shortages and increased demand for services in these areas.

Local health authorities are expected to review the data and consider strategies to improve access to care for residents in deprived areas.

articles.title: New Gentle Exercise Programme for Older Adults Launched
articles.excerpt: A new gentle exercise programme aimed at older adults has been launched to promote health and wellbeing in Rochdale.
articles.content_html:
A new gentle exercise programme for older adults has been launched in Rochdale. The initiative aims to encourage physical activity among seniors, promoting health and wellbeing in the community.

The programme is designed to be accessible and enjoyable, catering to the needs of older participants. Sessions will focus on low-impact exercises that can be performed safely and comfortably.

Local health officials have emphasised the importance of staying active as people age, highlighting the benefits of regular exercise for both physical and mental health.

The programme is expected to run weekly, providing a supportive environment for older adults to engage in physical activity and social interaction.

articles.title: Impact Gym Opens New Facility in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Impact Gym has opened a new facility in Rochdale, enhancing fitness options for local residents with state-of-the-art equipment and diverse classes.
articles.content_html:
Impact Gym has opened a new facility in Rochdale, providing additional fitness options for local residents. The gym is located on the outskirts of the town centre and aims to cater to a diverse range of fitness enthusiasts.

The new facility features state-of-the-art equipment and group classes. This expansion is part of the gym's commitment to promoting health and wellness in the community.

Members of the gym have expressed excitement about the new offerings, which include a variety of classes designed for different fitness levels.

The opening of the facility may increase foot traffic in the area, encouraging residents to visit and explore the new services available.

articles.title: Rochdale's STARS Adult Care Service Rated Good by Inspectors
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council's STARS adult care service has received a 'good' rating from the Care Quality Commission following its latest inspection, recognising its effective support for vulnerable residents.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council's STARS adult care service has been rated 'good' by the Care Quality Commission (CQC) following its latest inspection. The service supports over 1,200 vulnerable residents each year after they leave hospital, helping them maintain independence at home.

The CQC assessment found the STARS service to be well-led, delivering effective, caring, and responsive support for people living in Heywood, Middleton, the Pennines, and Rochdale. Inspectors praised staff for treating residents with kindness, empathy, and compassion, while assisting them in living healthier lives and maintaining their independence, choice, and control.

The report highlighted the service's commitment to placing people at the centre of decisions regarding their care, effective partnership working, and strong communication with families and external services. The STARS service has been operational since 2008 and has also contributed to reducing health inequalities and freeing up valuable hospital beds.

Councillor Shah Wazir, the council's cabinet member for adult care, expressed pride in the service, stating that the CQC report is a testament to the high-quality care provided by the STARS team. He acknowledged the importance of delivering services that help people remain independent in their preferred environments. The council's wider adult care service also received a good rating from the CQC, further underscoring the quality of care in the borough.

articles.title: Local Fitness Classes to Feature High Intensity Interval Training
articles.excerpt: A series of fitness classes featuring High Intensity Interval Training will be held in Rochdale, offering residents a chance to improve their fitness levels.
articles.content_html:
A series of fitness classes featuring High Intensity Interval Training will take place in Rochdale. The sessions aim to provide an effective workout for participants looking to enhance their fitness levels.

The classes, organised by Your Trust, will focus on high-intensity exercises designed to improve cardiovascular health and overall strength. Participants of all fitness levels are welcome to join.

In addition to High Intensity Interval Training, other fitness options such as core training will also be available, allowing residents to choose classes that suit their individual needs.

These initiatives are part of a broader effort to promote health and wellness within the community.

articles.title: Heads Together Initiative Launched in Rochdale Schools
articles.excerpt: A new initiative called Heads Together has been launched in Rochdale to support mental health in schools, providing resources and training for staff and students.
articles.content_html:
A new initiative called Heads Together has been launched in Rochdale to enhance mental health support in schools. The programme aims to provide resources and training for staff and students, focusing on creating a supportive environment for mental well-being.

Heads Together will involve workshops and training sessions designed to help schools address mental health issues effectively. The initiative seeks to equip educators with the tools necessary to identify and support students facing mental health challenges.

Local education leaders have expressed their support for the initiative, highlighting the importance of mental health awareness in schools.

Further details on the initiative will be shared with schools in the coming weeks.

articles.title: Your Trust Launches Body Balance Programme in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Your Trust has introduced a new Body Balance programme aimed at improving health and wellbeing in Rochdale. The initiative encourages community participation and offers various classes.
articles.content_html:
Your Trust has launched a new programme called Body Balance, designed to enhance health and wellbeing in the Rochdale area. The initiative aims to promote physical activity through a series of classes.

The Body Balance programme includes sessions tailored to different fitness levels, encouraging residents to participate and improve their overall health. Classes will be held at various locations across Rochdale.

Participants can engage in activities that focus on flexibility, strength, and relaxation, making it suitable for individuals of all ages.

For more information on the Body Balance programme and how to join, residents can visit the Your Trust website or contact their local community centre.

articles.title: Pilates Classes Now Available in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A new series of Pilates classes has been launched in Rochdale by Your Trust, designed to enhance fitness and promote overall wellbeing among participants.
articles.content_html:
A new series of Pilates classes has been launched in Rochdale by Your Trust. The classes aim to enhance fitness and promote overall wellbeing among participants.

Sessions will cater to various skill levels, ensuring that both beginners and experienced participants can benefit from the exercises. The classes will focus on core strength, flexibility, and posture improvement.

Classes are scheduled at convenient times throughout the week, making it easier for residents to incorporate them into their routines. Interested individuals can find more details on timings and registration on the Your Trust website.

This initiative provides residents with an opportunity to engage in healthy practices.

articles.title: Rochdale marks national day of memory for victims of honour-based abuse
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Borough Council has joined communities across the country in remembering victims of honour-based abuse and highlighting support available to people experiencing domestic violence.
articles.summary: The National Day of Memory for Victims of Honour-Based Abuse is marked on 14 July, the birthday of Shafilea Ahmed, whose death became one of the UK’s most widely recognised cases of so-called honour-based killing.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Borough Council has joined communities across the country in marking the National Day of Memory for Victims of Honour-Based Abuse.

The annual day of remembrance takes place on 14 July and provides an opportunity to remember those whose lives have been lost through so-called honour-based abuse and to call for collective action to prevent further harm.

Remembering Shafilea Ahmed

The date was chosen because 14 July would have been the birthday of Shafilea Ahmed, a young woman whose life was taken after she was perceived to have brought shame upon her family.

Her case has become closely associated with national efforts to raise awareness of honour-based abuse, forced marriage and the risks faced by people subjected to family or community control.

Rochdale Borough Council said an estimated 12 lives are lost to so-called honour killings each year, although the true number is believed to be higher.

Support available in Rochdale

The council has reminded residents that its domestic abuse service supports people of any background or gender who are affected by domestic violence or abuse.

Support is also available for individuals who want help to change abusive behaviour.

Residents can contact the Rochdale domestic abuse service on 0161 507 9609 or visit the Rochdale Borough Council domestic violence and abuse support page.

Anyone who is in immediate danger should call 999.

Mental-health support

People who need urgent mental-health advice can call NHS 111 and select the mental-health option where available.

Information about local services is also available through the council’s mental-health support directory.

This report is based on a public statement issued by Rochdale Borough Council to mark the National Day of Memory for Victims of Honour-Based Abuse on 14 July 2026.
source_names: Rochdale Borough Council mental-health support directory
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article discusses honour-based abuse, domestic violence and death. Anyone in immediate danger should call 999. Support services may change, so readers should confirm current contact arrangements through official providers.
